resistance_model.law: R(E) = R_10lux * (E / 10lux)^(-gamma), gamma ~ 0.7 (log-log slope over 10..100 lux); dark (0 lux) R >= 1 MOhm
resistance_model.note: CdS cells are specified as a resistance range at 10 lux, not a single value; the gamma slope characterises the log-log R-vs-illuminance curve between 10 and 100 lux. In a build spec, the LDR is emitted as an ordinary resistor whose <value> is the resistance at the illuminance under test (read the range/gamma above). The 10 lux typical (10 kOhm) is used for the divider micro-verification.

source: Senba/Shenzhen GL5528 CdS photoresistor datasheet (GL55 series): 10 lux resistance 8-20 kOhm, dark resistance >= 1 MOhm, gamma100 ~ 0.7, peak 540 nm. https://www.handsontec.com/dataspecs/sensor/GL55-LDR.pdf
provenance_note: Behavioral, NOT a datasheet-exact photodiode/optical SPICE model. It is a parameterized resistor: the emitter produces a plain R card from <value>, and the divider math is what a build spec asserts. No light port, no spectral model -- illuminance is chosen by the spec author as the <value> at that illuminance.
